Interactive Strategy Analytics Dashboard

Generated from reports/backtest/trades.csv and reports/backtest/entry_candidates.csv. This dashboard is meant to show which zone scenarios have edge, not just a headline win rate.
Trades
3367.0
Sample is large enough to compare buckets, but still validate changes out-of-sample.

46.81%
Trades that closed above 0R
3R target hit rate
5.26%
Full modeled target, not all profitable exits

Expectancy per trade in risk units

Aggregate strategy contribution

33.23%
Touched +1R at any point
Reached +2R
12.50%
Touched +2R at any point
Avg MFE / MAE
1.80R / -3.90R
Best/worst unrealized movement
Strategy read:
  • Not all setups are equal: positive average-R buckets currently include Demand Breakdown / Continuation — Puts, Supply Breakout / Continuation — Calls.
  • Potential problem areas: negative average-R buckets currently include Supply Rejection — Puts, Demand Reversal / Hold — Calls. These may need stricter entry confirmation or separate rules.
  • Watchlist quality matters: Final/Actionable scenarios averaged 0.141R versus Developing scenarios at -0.008R.
  • Exit logic is separating outcomes: EMA protection averaged 0.761R while zone stops averaged -1.000R.
How to read this: A strategy is not a dice roll if certain scenario buckets consistently show positive average R while others do not. Focus first on average R, reached +1R/+2R, and MFE vs MAE; profit win rate alone can hide whether winners are too small or losses are too large.

Interactive Strategy Filters

Use this section for exploratory optimization. Filters recalculate instantly in the browser; no Python rerun needed. Watch sample size so you do not overfit a tiny subset.

Filtered average R by scenario

Shows whether the selected subset is being carried by specific scenario types.

Filtered target progression

Touched milestone, not necessarily exit point.

Filtered By Scenario

Filtered By Watchlist Bucket

Filtered By Grade

Filtered By Exit Reason

Filter variable definitions
Watchlist bucketFinal/Actionable, Developing, or Research. Use this to test whether lower-confidence prep scenarios dilute results.
Setup gradeThe watchlist quality grade assigned before the intraday entry. Filtering out B/C tests whether lower-quality setups are hurting expectancy.
ScenarioThe supply/demand trade thesis, such as demand hold calls or supply breakout calls.
Entry kindSimplified entry family: continuation/breakout/breakdown versus reversal/rejection.
Zone typeDemand or supply. This tests whether one side is stronger in the sample.
Exit reasonHow the replay closed the trade. This is mostly diagnostic; do not optimize by exit reason as a pre-entry filter because you would not know it beforehand.

Reversal/Rejection Diagnostics

Purpose: This audits whether Demand Reversal / Hold — Calls and Supply Rejection — Puts are genuinely weak, or whether the replay is entering/managing them differently than a live trader would. Continuation setups remain excluded because they can stay more permissive.

Exit Path Audit

Purpose: This section checks whether replay scoring is matching live trade-path management. It does not change the watchlist. It highlights same-candle stop/target ambiguity, trades stopped after reaching +R milestones, MFE/MAE until exit versus full-day movement, and reversal-specific management simulations.

Variable glossary

VariableMeaning
Profit win ratePercent of simulated trades that ended with r_multiple > 0. A +0.20R 9EMA exit counts as profitable; it does not require the 3R target.
Target hit ratePercent of trades that reached the modeled full target, usually +3R in this backtest.
Average RAverage trade result measured in risk units. +0.25R means the average trade made one quarter of the initial risk.
Total RSum of all R-multiples. Useful for overall strategy contribution, but affected by trade count.
MFEMaximum favorable excursion: the best unrealized R the trade reached after entry. This shows whether the idea moved in our favor even if the exit did not capture it.
MAEMaximum adverse excursion: the worst unrealized R against the trade. Large negative MAE means the stop/position management needs review.
Reached +1R/+2R/+3RWhether price touched that R milestone at any point after entry, not necessarily where the trade exited.
9EMA protectionRisk-management exit after the trade has moved enough to justify protection. This is separate from the original thesis target.
Edge readA plain-English bucket from average R, profit win rate, and sample size. It is a guide, not a statistical proof.
